reason: The missing hint `getParameterizedSqlStatement` in s6 has low user impact. `ParameterizedSqlPStatement` (the return type and central class of the feature) is already present in s6 hints, so section s6 will be retrieved for any query involving that class. A direct search for the factory method name alone is an uncommon query pattern — developers typically search by feature concept or class name. The section content also contains the method name, so semantic retrieval would surface it regardless. The fix did not persist because the Phase E output was truncated (the JSON in the Phase E log is cut off mid-content at `"```java\nPar..."`), meaning the fix agent's response was incomplete and the update was not applied.

findings_assessment.justification: `ParameterizedSqlPStatement` — the central class of this entire feature — is already present in s6 hints. A user searching for the factory method `getParameterizedSqlStatement` in isolation is an atypical query; they would more likely search by the class name or the feature concept (object field registration). The section content contains the method name, so semantic retrieval compensates for the missing hint. Fix did not persist because Phase E output was truncated mid-JSON, leaving the file unchanged.
